headline: Why Did NFLX, ORCL, SPCX Stocks Slump To 52-Week Lows Last Week?
summary: Netflix, Oracle and SpaceX declined last week amid lower-than-expected future growth, mounting debt concerns, and a decline in sentiment after a historic IPO.

content.title: Why Did NFLX, ORCL, SPCX Stocks Slump To 52-Week Lows Last Week?

headline: Leading investment bank lifts Arm target to $320 as AI drives surge in CPU demand
summary: Jefferies has raised its price target on Arm Holdings PLC (NASDAQ:ARM) to $320 from $290, arguing that orders for the chip designer's AGI CPU have risen further since full-year results as agentic artificial intelligence fuels demand for processors. The broker repeated its 'buy' rating on the...

headline: Why Dan Ives Maintains His Sky High Target on IBM After Its Largest Crash Ever
summary: IBM just suffered its worst single-week collapse since 1968, yet one prominent analyst refuses to budge from a price target that implies more than 60% upside. What he sees in the wreckage that the sellers are missing could reshape the entire enterprise AI trade.

headline: Burry covers half of his Oracle short bet
summary: Oracle shares are a direct focal point: the stock touched a fresh 52-week low of $121.50 on Friday, July 18. That level sits roughly 63% below Oracle’s all-time high of $345.72, a collapse that has made Burry’s trade one of the more consequential short calls of 2026.

content.title: Burry covers half of his Oracle short bet
content.summary: Oracle shares are a direct focal point: the stock touched a fresh 52-week low of $121.50 on Friday, July 18. That level sits roughly 63% below Oracle’s all-time high of $345.72, a collapse that has made Burry’s trade one of the more consequential short calls of 2026.

headline: AMD Stock Rises on Vote of Confidence From a Big AI Chip Customer
summary: Microsoft plans to use AMD’s Helios AI infrastructure platform and EPYC data-center processors to power more of its Azure cloud-computing services, the two companies said Monday.  AMD stock jumped 5.1% to $521.23 on Monday after the market open, while the  rose 0.7%.  A combination of Microsoft and AMD technology will power new tools for data processing, electronic design automation, and inference, or running AI models.
